name: Market Stall
calculationsAnalysis: The Market Stall represents the entry point to commercial ventures in Renaissance Venice. Historically, these simple structures required minimal investment (150,000 ducats) but provided steady daily income (8,000 ducats) with low maintenance (1,000 ducats). This creates a reasonable ROI of about 1.3% per cycle, balancing accessibility for new players while requiring multiple cycles to recoup the initial investment. The employment capacity of 1 reflects the family-operated nature of these businesses, while construction time of 1 day (86400000ms) allows new players to quickly establish basic income streams.
shortDescription: Simple open-air selling space for basic goods and produce.
fullDescription: The humble beginning of many Venetian merchants, these wooden stalls provide a simple but effective way to sell goods directly to the public. Typically clustered in market areas or along busy streets, market stalls can be assembled daily and feature awnings for protection from sun and rain.
flavorText: A good location, a keen eye, and a loud voice—the three essentials of the successful market vendor.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Must be placed in designated market areas or along commercial streets
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Requires open plaza or street access
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases foot traffic in immediate area

inputResources.Various Trade Goods: 5-10 units per day depending on type

outputResources.Sales Revenue: 8-15 ducats per day depending on location and goods

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian market stall under construction, simple wooden framework being assembled in town square, partial awning of striped fabric, small counter being built, basic wooden structure, crates ready to display goods, simple tools for assembly, very quick construction process, workers securing support posts, historically accurate but simple design, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, warm wood textures

specialAbilities: Flexible location - can be moved for a fee

soundDesign.activitySounds: Haggling, coins clinking, goods being arranged

seasonalEffects.winter: Additional wind protection, brazier for warmth
seasonalEffects.rain: Additional awning coverage, goods better protected

name: Bottega
unlockCondition: 1,000 ducats total wealth, any Guild membership
calculationsAnalysis: The Bottega represents a step up in commercial investment with a construction cost of 600,000 ducats and income of 11,000 ducats, providing a 1.8% return per cycle. The maintenance cost of 4,000 ducats (36% of income) reflects the more complex structure and workshop equipment. With an employment capacity of 3, it creates a significant jump in workforce requirements, representing the master craftsman and apprentices typical of Renaissance workshops. The construction time of 2 days (172800000ms) creates a meaningful progression from the Market Stall while remaining accessible to early-game players. This building serves as a bridge between simple retail and specialized production.

shortDescription: Specialized shop with workshop in back, common for artisans and merchants.
fullDescription: The backbone of Venetian retail, these versatile shops combine a front retail area with a rear workshop where goods are produced or customized. With large shutters that open to the street during business hours, botteghe create an inviting shopping environment while allowing craftspeople to work efficiently in the same location.
flavorText: The hands create in the back, the voice sells in the front—the perfect marriage of craft and commerce.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Best suited to commercial streets in any district
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases property value of adjacent buildings by 5%, creates steady foot traffic

inputResources.Raw Materials: 15-25 units per day depending on specialization

name: Merceria
unlockCondition: 2,500 ducats total wealth, connection to Textile Guild
calculationsAnalysis: The Merceria's economic profile reflects the importance of textile retail in Venice's economy. With a construction cost of 1,000,000 ducats and income of 15,000 ducats (1.5% return), it represents a specialized investment with modest but reliable returns. The maintenance cost of 6,000 ducats (40% of income) balances profitability with realistic overhead for a specialized shop. The employment capacity of 4 reflects the staff needed to handle, display, and sell valuable textiles. The construction time of 3 days (259200000ms) represents the more refined construction and specialized fixtures required. This building creates a strategic choice for players to specialize in the lucrative textile trade, with appropriate guild requirements reflecting historical trade organization.

The Merceria's economic profile reflects the importance of textile retail in Venice's economy. shortDescription: Specialized textile and fabric shop offering a variety of imported and local cloths.
fullDescription: Named after Venice's famous shopping street, these specialized shops are the center of the textile trade. Offering everything from simple local woolens to exotic silks from the East, a merceria features dramatic fabric displays, specialized measuring counters, and often a separate area for noble clients. These shops connect Venice's vital textile industry directly to consumers.
flavorText: Under these silks lie the true fabric of Venice—commerce that spans from humble wool to threads of gold.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Premium locations in San Marco, Rialto, or Castello
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases property value of adjacent buildings by 8%, attracts wealthy clientele

name: Apothecary
unlockCondition: 3,000 ducats total wealth, Medical or Spice Guild connection
calculationsAnalysis: The Apothecary balances specialized commercial investment with essential community services. The construction cost of 1,250,000 ducats reflects the specialized equipment and fixtures required, while the income of 14,000 ducats (1.1% return) represents the steady demand for medicines and treatments. The maintenance cost of 7,500 ducats (54% of income) creates a tighter profit margin than other shops, reflecting the costs of importing and maintaining exotic ingredients. The employment capacity of 3 represents the apothecary and assistants typical of Renaissance medical shops. The construction time of 3 days (259200000ms) reflects the specialized nature of the building. This building provides both direct income and community health benefits, encouraging strategic placement near residential areas.

shortDescription: Medicine and herb shop offering remedies, cosmetics, and imported spices.
fullDescription: Combining pharmacy, perfumery, and exotic goods shop, the Venetian apothecary is a vital community resource. The distinctive interior features walls lined with labeled ceramic jars, a preparation counter, and often a small garden for fresh herbs. Offering everything from fever remedies to beauty treatments, these shops benefit from Venice's position as a gateway for Eastern medical knowledge and ingredients.
flavorText: Between these jars lies the boundary between East and West, between ancient wisdom and modern commerce, between life and death itself.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: All districts, particularly near residential areas
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases health of nearby citizens, slight exotic aroma in vicinity

name: Luxury Showroom
calculationsAnalysis: The Luxury Showroom represents high-end retail with substantial investment and returns. The construction cost of 3,000,000 ducats reflects the premium materials and prestigious location required, while the income of 35,000 ducats (1.2% return) represents the high margins on luxury goods. The maintenance cost of 20,000 ducats (57% of income) reflects the high standards of upkeep and security required. The employment capacity of 5 represents the specialized staff needed to serve elite clientele. The construction time of 5 days (432000000ms) creates a meaningful progression tier for advanced players. This building creates a strategic choice to invest in high-end retail with appropriate wealth and connection requirements reflecting the exclusive nature of luxury commerce in Renaissance Venice.
shortDescription: High-end display space for luxury goods catering to nobility and wealthy merchants.
fullDescription: The pinnacle of Venetian retail, these elegant spaces showcase the finest products Venice has to offer. Unlike crowded market shops, luxury showrooms feature carefully curated displays, private consultation areas, and by-appointment service for elite clients. Often specializing in a particular luxury good—Murano glass, jewelry, imported rarities—these establishments cultivate an exclusive atmosphere that justifies premium prices.
flavorText: Here we do not simply sell objects, but rather the very essence of refinement and distinction that separates the truly cultured from the merely wealthy.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Premium locations only: San Marco, Grand Canal frontage, or Rialto
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Benefits from proximity to other luxury establishments
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Significantly increases property value of surrounding buildings, attracts wealthy visitors

name: Fondaco dei Tedeschi
unlockCondition: 15,000 ducats total wealth, German merchant connections, Senate approval
calculationsAnalysis: The Fondaco dei Tedeschi represents a major commercial investment with significant returns. The construction cost of 7,500,000 ducats reflects the massive scale and institutional nature of this trading complex, while the income of 75,000 ducats (1% return) represents the substantial commercial activity it facilitates. The maintenance cost of 35,000 ducats (47% of income) balances profitability with the reality of maintaining a large commercial complex. The employment capacity of 40 reflects the administrative staff, guards, and workers needed to operate this international trading center. The construction time of 15 days (1296000000ms) represents a major project appropriate for advanced players. This building creates a strategic investment in international trade with appropriate requirements reflecting the political and diplomatic nature of establishing foreign trading posts in Renaissance Venice.
shortDescription: German merchant trading hall combining warehouse, accommodation, and commercial offices.
fullDescription: A cornerstone of Venice's international trade, this massive complex serves as headquarters for German merchants. Combining warehouse facilities, living quarters, offices, and trading halls, the fondaco creates a self-contained community for foreign traders under Venetian oversight. State regulations require all German goods to pass through this building, making it a vital link to Northern European markets while allowing careful tax collection.
flavorText: Between these walls the languages of north and south mingle, as do the coins of a dozen cities—all under the watchful eye of the Serenissima.

locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Requires major canal access, proximity to commercial district
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Creates international commercial zone, increases nearby property values by 20%

name: Eastern Merchant House
unlockCondition: 20,000 ducats total wealth, Eastern trade connections
calculationsAnalysis: The Eastern Merchant House balances substantial investment with lucrative Eastern trade returns. The construction cost of 5,000,000 ducats reflects the specialized architecture and prestigious location required, while the income of 60,000 ducats (1.2% return) represents the high-value Eastern luxury goods trade. The maintenance cost of 30,000 ducats (50% of income) reflects the costs of maintaining cultural specialists and exotic goods handling. The employment capacity of 20 represents the staff needed to manage complex Eastern trade relationships. The construction time of 10 days (864000000ms) represents a significant project for advanced players. This building creates a strategic investment in the lucrative Eastern trade routes that were historically crucial to Venice's commercial dominance, with appropriate requirements reflecting the specialized knowledge and connections needed.
shortDescription: Trading post specialized in Eastern goods, combining warehouse, offices, and cultural showcase.
fullDescription: Venice's gateway to the riches of the East, these specialized trading houses focus on goods from the Ottoman Empire, Persia, and beyond. Featuring architecture and decoration influenced by Eastern styles, these buildings provide both commercial facilities and cultural spaces where Eastern and Western merchants can negotiate. Often housing translators and cultural experts, they facilitate the flow of exotic goods that made Venice wealthy.
flavorText: Here the silk road reaches its final destination, as do the dreams of a thousand caravans—and the nightmares of Venice's competitors.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Waterfront locations, particularly in Castello or Cannaregio
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Requires dock access, benefits from proximity to spice markets
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Creates exotic atmosphere, slightly increases crime risk but significantly increases commercial activity

name: Broker's Office
unlockCondition: 5,000 ducats total wealth, Merchant Guild membership
calculationsAnalysis: The Broker's Office represents specialized commercial services with modest infrastructure but significant returns. The construction cost of 1,000,000 ducats reflects the secure, professional space required, while the income of 20,000 ducats (2% return) represents the lucrative fees earned from facilitating transactions without inventory costs. The maintenance cost of 7,500 ducats (37.5% of income) creates a healthy profit margin reflecting the low-overhead nature of brokerage. The employment capacity of 4 represents the broker and administrative staff. The construction time of 2 days (172800000ms) reflects the relatively simple construction requirements. This building creates a strategic option for players to participate in trade without direct goods handling, reflecting Venice's sophisticated commercial ecosystem where intermediaries played crucial roles.
shortDescription: Specialized office for trade intermediaries who connect buyers with sellers for a commission.
fullDescription: The oil that keeps Venice's commercial machinery running smoothly, brokers facilitate transactions without ever taking ownership of goods. These offices feature secure document storage, private meeting rooms for confidential negotiations, and often a public-facing area where potential clients can make inquiries. Brokers specialize by industry or trade route, building networks of connections that make them invaluable intermediaries.
flavorText: The shrewd broker sells not silk nor spice nor silver, but something far more valuable: the perfect connection, made at precisely the right moment.

locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Benefits from proximity to related industry offices
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases efficiency of nearby commercial buildings by 5%

productionInformation.storageCapacity: Minimal physical storage, extensive document storage

name: Customs House
unlockCondition: Senate appointment or 25,000 ducats with government connections
calculationsAnalysis: The Customs House represents the intersection of government authority and commercial regulation. The construction cost of 4,000,000 ducats reflects the substantial secure infrastructure required, while the high income of 100,000 ducats (2.5% return) represents the significant tax revenue generated through import/export duties. The maintenance cost of 25,000 ducats (25% of income) creates a substantial profit margin appropriate for a revenue-generating government institution. The employment capacity of 30 represents the officials, guards, and clerks needed to process goods and collect duties. The construction time of 7 days (604800000ms) reflects the substantial construction requirements. This building creates a strategic investment in government infrastructure with appropriate high-level requirements reflecting the political nature of customs control in Renaissance Venice.
shortDescription: Official goods inspection and taxation facility operated under government authority.
fullDescription: The gateway through which all legal goods must pass, the customs house combines administrative offices, inspection facilities, and secure storage. Government officials carefully record imported and exported goods, calculate appropriate taxes, and inspect for contraband. These imposing buildings represent Venice's strict commercial regulations, which helped maintain quality standards while generating substantial revenue for the Republic.
flavorText: Through these doors pass the riches of the world—and a healthy portion remains behind for the glory of the Serenissima.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Must be positioned at key harbor entrance points
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Requires direct deep water access for ships
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Decreases illegal trade in area, increases formal commercial activity

outputResources.Tax Revenue: 150-300 ducats per day to state treasury

name: Weighing Station
unlockCondition: Government license or 8,000 ducats with official connections
calculationsAnalysis: The Weighing Station balances government service with modest revenue generation. The construction cost of 1,750,000 ducats reflects the specialized equipment and official infrastructure required, while the income of 22,500 ducats (1.3% return) represents the fees collected for standardized measurements. The maintenance cost of 12,500 ducats (55.6% of income) creates a modest profit margin appropriate for a semi-governmental service. The employment capacity of 8 represents the officials and workers needed to operate measurement equipment and maintain records. The construction time of 3 days (259200000ms) reflects the specialized construction requirements. This building creates a strategic investment in commercial infrastructure that benefits the entire trading system while generating modest direct returns, reflecting Venice's sophisticated approach to standardization and commercial regulation.
shortDescription: Official product measurement facility ensuring accurate weights and measures for commerce.
fullDescription: Accuracy in commerce was essential to Venice's reputation, and these official stations provided standardized weights and measures for all significant transactions. Featuring precisely calibrated scales, volume measures, and length standards, these government-regulated facilities prevented fraud while collecting small fees for their services. The official certification they provided gave merchants confidence in their transactions.
flavorText: A gram too light or an inch too short may seem trivial, until one calculates the difference across a thousand transactions—then one understands why the Republic is so precise in its measures.

locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Requires good access for carts and porters
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases efficiency and trust in local commercial transactions
